questions.question: Define a polynomial and explain its general form with examples.
questions.answer: A polynomial is an algebraic expression consisting of variables and constants with non-negative integer powers of the variables. The general form of a polynomial in one variable x is aₙxⁿ + aₙ₋₁xⁿ⁻¹ + … + a₁x + a₀. Examples include 2x² + 3x − 1 and 5x − 4.

questions.question: Explain the degree of a polynomial with suitable examples.
questions.answer: The degree of a polynomial is the highest power of the variable with a non-zero coefficient. For example, the degree of 3x² + 2x + 1 is 2, while the degree of 5x³ − x is 3.

questions.question: Classify polynomials based on their degree.
questions.answer: Polynomials are classified based on degree as constant (degree 0), linear (degree 1), quadratic (degree 2), cubic (degree 3) and so on.

questions.question: Explain linear polynomials with examples.
questions.answer: A linear polynomial is a polynomial of degree one. Its general form is ax + b, where a ≠ 0. Examples include 2x + 3 and −5x + 7.

questions.question: Explain quadratic polynomials with examples.
questions.answer: A quadratic polynomial is a polynomial of degree two. Its general form is ax² + bx + c, where a ≠ 0. Examples include x² − 3x + 2 and 2x² + x − 5.

questions.question: Explain constant polynomials with examples.
questions.answer: A constant polynomial has no variable term and its degree is zero. Examples include 5, −3 and 10.

questions.question: Explain zero polynomial and its degree.
questions.answer: A zero polynomial has all coefficients equal to zero. Its degree is not defined because it has no non-zero term.

questions.question: Define the zeroes of a polynomial.
questions.answer: The zeroes of a polynomial are the values of the variable for which the polynomial becomes zero.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between the degree of a polynomial and the number of zeroes.
questions.answer: The maximum number of zeroes of a polynomial is equal to its degree. A polynomial may have fewer zeroes than its degree.

questions.question: Explain how the graph of a polynomial helps in finding its zeroes.
questions.answer: The zeroes of a polynomial are the x-coordinates of the points where the graph intersects the x-axis.

questions.question: Explain the graph of a linear polynomial.
questions.answer: The graph of a linear polynomial is a straight line. It intersects the x-axis at exactly one point.

questions.question: Explain the graph of a quadratic polynomial.
questions.answer: The graph of a quadratic polynomial is a parabola. It may intersect the x-axis at two points, one point or no point.

questions.question: Explain why a polynomial cannot have negative powers of variables.
questions.answer: Polynomials are defined only for non-negative integer powers of variables. Negative powers make the expression non-polynomial.

questions.question: Explain the standard form of a polynomial.
questions.answer: The standard form of a polynomial arranges terms in descending powers of the variable.

questions.question: Explain monomial, binomial and trinomial with examples.
questions.answer: A monomial has one term, a binomial has two terms and a trinomial has three terms. Examples are 3x, x + 2 and x² + 3x + 1 respectively.

questions.question: Explain why the zero polynomial has no defined degree.
questions.answer: Because all its coefficients are zero and there is no highest power to determine the degree.

questions.question: Explain the significance of zeroes of a polynomial.
questions.answer: Zeroes indicate where the polynomial equals zero and help in understanding the behaviour of the graph.

questions.question: Explain why a polynomial of degree one has at most one zero.
questions.answer: Because its graph is a straight line that can intersect the x-axis only once.

questions.question: Explain why a polynomial of degree two can have at most two zeroes.
questions.answer: Because a parabola can intersect the x-axis at most at two points.

questions.question: Explain the use of polynomials in daily life.
questions.answer: Polynomials are used to model area, profit-loss, motion and many real-life situations involving relationships between quantities.

questions.question: Explain why all algebraic expressions are not polynomials.
questions.answer: Only expressions with non-negative integer powers of variables are polynomials. Others violate polynomial rules.

questions.question: Explain p(0) for a polynomial p(x).
questions.answer: p(0) gives the constant term of the polynomial because all variable terms become zero.

questions.question: Explain the importance of degree in a polynomial.
questions.answer: The degree determines the number of zeroes and the shape of the graph.

questions.question: Explain why a polynomial graph is continuous.
questions.answer: Because it has no breaks or gaps in its graph.

questions.question: Explain the role of polynomials in mathematics.
questions.answer: Polynomials form the foundation for algebra, equations, calculus and mathematical modelling.
